2008-12-01.title: NATO Duel Centers on Georgia and Ukraine
2008-12-01.abstract: The United States is eager to put Georgia and Ukraine on a path to NATO membership, but other alliance members are loath to rile Russia.

2008-12-01.title: Panel Fears Use of Unconventional Weapon
2008-12-01.abstract: The Congressionally mandated panel found that unconventional threats are fast outpacing the defenses arrayed to confront them.

2008-12-01.title: Deadly Nigeria Clashes Subside
2008-12-01.abstract: Soldiers recently wrested control of the streets from armed Christian and Muslim gangs that had roamed the city, in the worst religious violence Nigeria has seen in four years.

2008-12-01.title: Chávez Again Seeks to End Term Limits
2008-12-01.abstract: The move, which would have to be approved by a national referendum, comes a year after voters rejected a constitutional overhaul that included a similar measure.

2008-12-01.title: Mediator Says Ransom Deal Has Been Reached for Pirated Ukrainian Freighter
2008-12-01.abstract: The mediator said that the only thing left was planning the transfer of money and handover of the ship, which was captured by Somali pirates two months ago.

2008-12-02.title: A Premier With a Hand in TV News Sues His Journalist Critics
2008-12-02.abstract: Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi’s lawyer said Italian newspapers rarely punished erring journalists so public figures had to defend their names in court.

2008-12-02.title: Afghan Strategy Poses Stiff Challenge for Obama
2008-12-02.abstract: Experts caution that a troop increase in Afghanistan is unlikely to lead to the sort of turnaround seen in Iraq.

2008-12-02.title: Germany Aims to Guide the West’s Ties to Russia
2008-12-02.abstract: Germany hopes to counter Russia’s renewed militarism by promoting economic development and stability.

2008-12-02.title: Bangkok Airport Protests Threaten Thai Economy
2008-12-02.abstract: As they allowed empty planes to depart, protesters said they would focus their demonstrations on the airports.

2008-12-02.title: India-Pakistan Tensions Grow
2008-12-02.abstract: Indian officials said that Pakistanis were responsible and must be punished for last week's terrorist attacks.

2008-12-03.title: Thai Court Disbands Ruling Party
2008-12-03.abstract: Antigovernment protesters declared an end to their blockade of Bangkok's airports after a court disbanded the ruling party.

2008-12-03.title: Afghan Refugees Return Home but Find Only a Life of Desperation
2008-12-03.abstract: The situation in Afghan refugee settlements is dire, as government and international aid groups lack the capacity to confront the approaching winter.

2008-12-03.title: Jews of Mumbai, a Tiny and Eclectic Group, Suddenly Reconsider Their Serene Existence
2008-12-03.abstract: Mumbai’s 4,000 Jewish residents have been left badly shaken by the terrorist assault on Nariman House.

2008-12-03.title: British Balance Benefit vs. Cost of Latest Drugs
2008-12-03.abstract: Skyrocketing health care prices have led other countries to follow Britain’s example by asking how much life is worth.

2008-12-03.title: Head of State Tackles Crisis in Canada
2008-12-03.abstract: The governor general of Canada said that she would cut short a state visit to Europe and return here as a coalition of opposition parties sought to unseat the Conservative government.

2008-12-04.title: Britain’s Parliament Opens With Uproar Over Police Raid
2008-12-04.abstract: The ceremony at which Queen Elizabeth formally opens Parliament was overshadowed by an angry dispute over a counterterrorism raid on an opposition member’s office.

2008-12-04.title: Money to Fight Drug Gangs Is Released to Mexico
2008-12-04.abstract: The U.S. formally released the first part of a $400 million aid package, a sign of how much more involved the United States is becoming in Mexico’s brutal drug war.

2008-12-04.title: Clash in Iraq Over a Plan for Councils Intensifies
2008-12-04.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki on Wednesday rejected Kurdish arguments that he could not create new tribal councils that would answer to his government.

2008-12-04.title: State Finds (or Buys) Some Peace in Nigeria
2008-12-04.abstract: Nigeria’s Delta State has accomplished a rare measure of peace by drawing militants into the government and making sure they are awarded valuable contracts from oil companies.

2008-12-04.title: Council Backs Idea to Indict Sudan Leader
2008-12-04.abstract: Members of the Security Council generally expressed support for moving ahead with the possible indictment of President Omar Hassan al-Bashir of Sudan.

2008-12-05.title: Strangers May Cheer You Up, Study Says
2008-12-05.abstract: A new study found that happiness is more contagious than previously thought.

2008-12-05.title: Strangers May Cheer You Up, Study Says
2008-12-05.abstract: A new study found that happiness is more contagious than previously thought.

2008-12-05.title: Gene Test Shows Spain’s Jewish and Muslim Mix
2008-12-05.abstract: A study of genetic signatures has provided new evidence of the mass conversions of Sephardic Jews and Muslims to Catholicism in the 14th and 15th centuries.

2008-12-05.title: A Water Wonderland, From an Unlikely Source
2008-12-05.abstract: Cambodia is reviving its vast network of canals — built under the Khmer Rouge by millions of forced laborers — to capitalize on the world’s increasing demand for rice.

2008-12-05.title: France Introduces a $33 Billion Economic Stimulus Plan
2008-12-05.abstract: The plan includes aid to the country’s ailing auto industry, which employs 10 percent of the French work force, and sets aside money for the construction industry.

2008-12-06.title: $100 Million in Jewels Stolen From Paris Store
2008-12-06.abstract: A savvy band of jewel thieves, some posing as women, stole diamonds, rings and watches valued at more than $100 million from a Harry Winston boutique.

2008-12-06.title: Zimbabwe: Pressure on Mugabe
2008-12-06.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice said Friday that it was “well past time” for President Robert Mugabe of Zimbabwe to go.

2008-12-06.title: Sweden: Bosnian Denied Release
2008-12-06.abstract: The only woman to be convicted of war crimes in the Balkans, Biljana Plavsic, has had her request for an early release rejected for the second time by the government of Sweden.

2008-12-06.title: Somali Refugees Land Near Yemen
2008-12-06.abstract: More than 100 refugees fleeing Somalia were forced off a small fishing boat early Friday near a Yemeni coastal village, the humanitarian group Doctors Without Borders said.

2008-12-06.title: Sri Lankan Army Is Pushing for End to 25-Year War Against the Tamil Rebels
2008-12-06.abstract: With the country’s military making its deepest push into rebel territory in a decade, Asia’s longest-running civil war appears to be edging closer to a military solution.

2008-12-07.title: A Tribe in Brazil Struggles at the Intersection of Drugs and Cultures
2008-12-07.abstract: The Tikuna Indians are struggling to keep young people from losing themselves in the vices of the white man’s world and from destroying what is left of their culture.

2008-12-07.title: 6 Are Killed Amid Violence Across Iraq
2008-12-07.abstract: Violence on Saturday, primarily aimed at Iraqi civilians, police recruits and members of Awakening Councils, left at least six people dead in parts of Iraq.

2008-12-07.title: Revamping Pakistan Aid Expected in Report
2008-12-07.abstract: The Bush administration is preparing a strategy review aimed at reversing the gains that militants have made in Afghanistan and Pakistan.

2008-12-07.title: Shaman Channels 12th Century but Adapts to 21st
2008-12-07.abstract: As Taiwan’s economy has developed and its population urbanized, some jitongs — shamans who dispense advice while said to be possessed by a spirit — are changing with the times.

2008-12-07.title: Police Focus on Two Men in Mumbai Attacks
2008-12-07.abstract: Indian police said they have yet to determine what role two Indians arrested on Friday might have played in aiding militants in the three-day siege of Mumbai.

2008-12-08.title: U.S. Prosecutor Goes to Iraq to Work on Blackwater Case
2008-12-08.abstract: A prosecutor working on the case against five Blackwater guards indicted in connection with a 2007 shooting in Baghdad will be meeting with victims’ families this week, Iraqi officials said.

2008-12-08.title: Pakistan’s Spies Aided Group Tied to Mumbai Siege
2008-12-08.abstract: Lashkar-e-Taiba has gained strength in recent years with the help of Pakistan’s main spy service, American intelligence officials say.

2008-12-08.title: Muslims in India Put Aside Grievances to Repudiate Terrorism
2008-12-08.abstract: Throngs marched through Mumbai and other cities to condemn terrorism and proclaim loyalty to the nation.

2008-12-08.title: Plan to Deepen Economic Ties Between India and Pakistan Is on Hold
2008-12-08.abstract: On a long road of rivalry and reconciliation, how long the current tension will last or where it will lead is unclear.

2008-12-08.title: Debate Rages in Peru: Was a Lost City Ever Lost?
2008-12-08.abstract: Not only may Hiram Bingham not be quite the heroic pioneer that he has been portrayed as, but it may be that the Lost City of the Incas was never really lost.

2008-12-09.title: A Hole in the Ground Erupts, to Estonia’s Delight
2008-12-09.abstract: Exhaling puffs of vapor in the slanting light, the famed Witch’s Well of Tuhala erupted for the first time in three years.

2008-12-09.title: Venezuelan Given 15 Months in Suitcase of Cash Scandal
2008-12-09.abstract: The businessman was involved in an elaborate plot by Venezuelan and Argentine officials to cover up the origin and destination of a suitcase containing $800,000.

2008-12-09.title: Financial Crisis Tames Demand for World’s Oldest Service
2008-12-09.abstract: While prostitution may be one of the most recession-proof businesses, brothel owners in Europe and the United States are feeling the recession’s pinch.

2008-12-09.title: Liberal Party’s Leader in Canada Says He’ll Quit Early
2008-12-09.abstract: Just days after it appeared he might become prime minister, Stéphane Dion said he would accelerate his previously announced departure.

2008-12-09.title: Killings in Drug War in Mexico Double in ’08
2008-12-09.abstract: Mexico’s attorney general tied the increase in deaths to a battle for control among cartels and a power vacuum created by high-profile arrests and seizures.

2008-12-10.title: Blackwater? So Many Others.
2008-12-10.abstract: I did not feel anything going to Nisour Square. I consider it something minor. It was only one site among so many where such things happened in Iraq, whether it was Americans or terrorists who did the shooting.

2008-12-10.title: Shedding Feudalism, a Channel Island Fights Over Its Future
2008-12-10.abstract: The tiny Channel Island of Sark, off the coast of France, is split between preserving its old ways or aligning with more modern ones.

2008-12-10.title: India Wants Pakistani Group Added to U.N.’s Terrorism List
2008-12-10.abstract: India contends the group Jamaat-ud-Dawa is virtually interchangeable with Lashkar-e-Taiba, the group believed to have carried out the terrorist attacks in Mumbai last month.

2008-12-10.title: Campaigns Get Under Way for Provincial Elections in Iraq
2008-12-10.abstract: The elections, which are scheduled for Jan. 31, kicked off as Iraqis celebrated the three-day Muslim holiday of Id al-Adha.

2008-12-10.title: Writer and Academic Is Poised to Lead Canada’s Liberal Party
2008-12-10.abstract: Michael Ignatieff, a former journalist, documentary producer and academic, became the effective leader of the Liberal Party of Canada.

2008-12-11.title: French Foreign Minister Voices Doubts on Human Rights Push
2008-12-11.abstract: Bernard Kouchner, known as a fierce proponent of human rights, admitted in an interview that “there is permanent contradiction between human rights and the foreign policy of a state, even in France.”

2008-12-11.title: Talking to Fireflies Before Their Flash Disappears
2008-12-11.abstract: Fireflies known for synchronous flashing along a riverbank in Thailand appear to be under threat from encroaching human development.

2008-12-11.title: Brazil Lays Some Blame on U.S. Pilots in Collision
2008-12-11.abstract: A new Brazilian report on the collision of an American business jet and a airliner in 2006 put part of the blame on the American pilots, but a U.S. report put the main responsibility on the Brazilian air traffic control system.

2008-12-11.title: An Israeli Party Tips Further Right as Its Leader Woos Centrists
2008-12-11.abstract: The list of candidates for Likud is notably more hawkish than its leader, Benjamin Netanyahu, making it harder for him to campaign on the promise to form a centrist coalition if elected.

2008-12-11.title: U.S. Joins Effort to Bar Claims on Iraqi Coffers
2008-12-11.abstract: The U.S. has joined an effort to block claims against Iraq stemming from Saddam Hussein’s rule.

2008-12-12.title: U.S. Presence at Conference on Climate Raises Hopes
2008-12-12.abstract: At a United Nations gathering in Poland, Senator John Kerry reassured delegates that the United States would take strong measures to combat climate change.

2008-12-12.title: An American’s Lament: ‘I Was Deported, Too’
2008-12-12.abstract: Between January and September, Mexico deported 350 Americans, some of them lawbreakers, but others merely travelers lacking proper paperwork.

2008-12-12.title: Pakistan Detains Founder of Group Suspected in Mumbai Attacks
2008-12-12.abstract: Pakistani authorities have detained the founder of Lashkar-e-Taiba, the group accused by India and the United States of conducting the Mumbai terror attacks.

2008-12-12.title: India Presses Pakistan on Terrorism but Finds Its Own Options Limited
2008-12-12.abstract: Indian officials lambasted Pakistan as the “epicenter” of terrorism, but seemed to rule out the prospect of a military confrontation.

2008-12-12.title: As Draft Ends, Polish Military Faces Struggle to Modernize
2008-12-12.abstract: Poland’s attempt to transform its military is occurring in a season of turmoil, as its soldiers have left Iraq and are expanding their presence in Afghanistan.

2008-12-13.title: Violence Brings Issues Plaguing Greece to the Surface
2008-12-13.abstract: Conversations with Athenians revealed great anxiety over the economy and a widespread feeling that they had been neglected and abandoned by a government they saw as corrupt.

2008-12-13.title: Britain: Fine, Keep Your Democracy
2008-12-13.abstract: Angry that the election on the island of Sark this week did not go their way, the island’s biggest investors announced that they would close all their projects and fire their 140 employees there.

2008-12-13.title: Italy: Flooding Paralyzes Rome
2008-12-13.abstract: Two days of nonstop rain swelled the Tiber River, putting Rome on flood alert on Friday.

2008-12-13.title: Russia: Mine Explosion Kills 12
2008-12-13.abstract: An explosion at a mine in the Murmansk region of northern Russia killed 12 people just before midnight on Thursday, a regional emergency spokeswoman said.

2008-12-13.title: Nigeria: Presidential Election Upheld
2008-12-13.abstract: The Supreme Court upheld the election of President Umaru Yar’Adua after more than a year and a half of legal battles and allegations of fraud by two opposition leaders.

2008-12-14.title: Trying to Stop Pollution From Killing a Lifeline
2008-12-14.abstract: A bank has committed to provide Indonesia with a $500 million, multiyear loan to finance a wide-ranging cleanup and rehabilitation plan of the Citarum River.

2008-12-14.title: Pakistan Says Indian Planes Entered Airspace
2008-12-14.abstract: The Pakistani government sought to play down the airspace violations as “inadvertent,” but it did not elaborate on how two separate breaches so far away could be unintended and coincidental.

2008-12-14.title: Iraqi Journalist Hurls Shoes at Bush and Denounces Him on TV as a ‘Dog’
2008-12-14.abstract: President Bush, on his fourth and final trip to Iraq, got a taste of dissent at a Baghdad press event Sunday.

2008-12-14.abstract: Atheer Kakan was in the press briefing when an Iraqi journalist threw his shoes at President Bush.

2008-12-15.title: Hamas, Showing Split, Hints It May Extend Truce
2008-12-15.abstract: Leaders left open the possibility of renewing a truce with Israel, putting themselves at odds with a statement by the group’s exiled political leader.

2008-12-15.abstract: Prime Minister Gordon Brown shuttled from India to Pakistan to try to tamp down tensions, but also demanded that Pakistan do more to curb terrorism.

2008-12-15.abstract: President Abdullahi Yusuf Ahmed announced that he was firing the prime minister, throwing Somalia’s beleaguered government into further disarray.

2008-12-15.title: Bush Affirms Commitment to Afghanistan
2008-12-15.abstract: President Bush made a surprise visit to Afghanistan and warned that while violence could continue to rise, the United States could not walk away from the conflict.

2008-12-15.title: A Defense Secretary at Work for 2 Commanders in Chief
2008-12-15.abstract: What was originally conceived as a goodbye tour for Robert M. Gates instead offered a preview of the president-elect’s strategy.

2008-12-16.title: Patrolling for Somali Pirates
2008-12-16.abstract: East Africa bureau chief Jeffrey Gettleman reports offshore on an Italian warship on efforts to combat piracy in the Gulf of Aden.

2008-12-16.title: Pirates Outmaneuver Warships Off Somalia
2008-12-16.abstract: More than a dozen warships from nearly the same number of countries have joined the hunt for Somali pirates, but the pirates do not seem especially deterred.

2008-12-16.title: A Portuguese Tradition Faces a Frozen Future
2008-12-16.abstract: As frozen bacalhau — a salted, cured and dried cod — replaces its fresh counterpart in the marketplace, some mourn the shift as a loss of part of the country’s culture.

2008-12-16.abstract: Prime Minister Gordon Brown authorized an increase in troop presence over the next 10 months, but gave no indication about troop levels beyond August.

2008-12-16.abstract: Much of the controversial barrier runs inside the West Bank, looping around some Jewish settlements, and the Palestinians had accused Israel of a land grab.

2008-12-17.title: Greece: Protesters Upstage Newscast
2008-12-17.abstract: A group of 40 protesters stormed a studio of Greece’s state NET television, breaking into a scheduled newscast to rally public support for antigovernment demonstrations.

2008-12-17.title: Allies See Germany Trying Bailout With a Thimble
2008-12-17.abstract: The country’s neighbors and many of its politicians are criticizing the government for spending plans that seem tight-fisted compared to what European countries or the U.S. are preparing.

2008-12-17.title: British Doctor Is Convicted in Failed Car Bombings
2008-12-17.abstract: A British doctor with family roots in Iraq was convicted in a case centering on a failed attack on Glasgow’s airport.

2008-12-17.title: Israel: Russians Killed in Bus Plunge
2008-12-17.abstract: At least two dozen Russian tour guides were killed and another two dozen injured Tuesday when a bus driving them to the southern resort of Eilat plunged down a ravine.

2008-12-17.title: U.N. Council Shows Support for U.S. Plan Against Piracy
2008-12-17.abstract: Overcoming misgivings among Security Council members about taking the fight against Somali pirates ashore, the U.S. won a unanimous endorsement for such measures.

2008-12-18.title: Thailand’s Winds Shift, Setting a Political Boss Adrift
2008-12-18.abstract: Without a sympathetic government in power to protect him, the former prime minister, Thaksin Shinawatra, has become more vulnerable to the workings of institutions he once commanded.

2008-12-18.title: Russia, Testing U.S. Sway, Offers Lebanon 10 Warplanes
2008-12-18.abstract: The 10 MIG-29 fighter jets would significantly upgrade their antiquated air force and serve as a slap to the United States, Lebanon’s main military partner.

2008-12-18.title: A Northern Ireland Town Is a Shoppers’ Paradise
2008-12-18.abstract: The euro has surged in value against the British pound, making prices in northern stores so irresistible that southerners are flocking over the border.

2008-12-18.title: Serial Crank Caller in Canada Ordered to Seek More Therapy
2008-12-18.abstract: Over 15 months, Marie-Ève Dean flooded Montreal’s 911 emergency line with more than 10,000 crank calls in a dialing marathon that sometimes blocked legitimate callers.

2008-12-18.title: Report Says Iraq May Ban Blackwater
2008-12-18.abstract: The security contractor’s license may not be renewed next year to protect American diplomats in Baghdad.

2008-12-19.title: A Gaza Truce Undone by Flaws May Be Revived by Necessity
2008-12-19.abstract: Officials and analysts on both sides say Israel and Hamas need the truce and will probably grope their way back to it — but how soon and after how much suffering?

2008-12-19.title: Wounds Heal, but Grief Lingers in Mumbai
2008-12-19.abstract: To travel down the corridors of the largest public hospital in Mumbai, India, is to witness physical wounds healing but mental ones that may linger for years.

2008-12-19.title: After 30 Years, Economic Perils on China’s Path
2008-12-19.abstract: Chinese leaders are facing a new era in which Deng Xiaoping’s export-led economic model faces challenges.

2008-12-19.title: China’s Long Boom Threatened
2008-12-19.abstract: The global recession has recently slowed China’s economic growth, creating political and social strains.

2008-12-19.title: A Corner of Indonesia, Sinking in a Sea of Mud
2008-12-19.abstract: A mud eruption triggered by exploratory drilling for natural gas has grown to be one of the largest mud volcanoes to have affected a populated area.

2008-12-20.title: Once a Political Riser, an Israeli Challenges His Country’s Identity
2008-12-20.abstract: Four years ago Avraham Burg — once talked about as a candidate for prime minister — not only walked away from politics, but also basically walked away from Zionism.

2008-12-20.title: Belgium: Prime Minister Offers to Quit
2008-12-20.abstract: Prime Minister Yves Leterme submitted his resignation on Friday amid allegations that his aides had tried to persuade justice officials not to interfere with the dismantling of a major bank.

2008-12-20.title: Congo Warlord Linked to Abuses Seeks Bigger Stage
2008-12-20.abstract: Gen. Laurent Nkunda, who opposes Congo’s president, has taken the nation to its most dangerous precipice in years.

2008-12-20.title: Kashmiris Weary of Violence Fight Back by Voting
2008-12-20.abstract: After years of insurgency, many in Kashmir — the disputed territory between Pakistan and India — are weary of fighting and are turning out in record numbers to vote.

2008-12-20.title: 24 Officers to Be Freed, Iraqi Says
2008-12-20.abstract: Iraqi’s interior minister publicly condemned his own government’s investigation, calling the accusations false and motivated purely by politics.

2008-12-21.title: Correction
2008-12-21.abstract: An article last Sunday about Cuba’s economic relations with Venezuela misstated, in some editions, the recently estimated size of Cuba’s offshore oil reserves, which have aroused speculation that Cuba, with Venezuelan help in building refineries, may become a regional oil-refining center. The reserves contain as many as 20 billion barrels, not 20 million.

2008-12-21.title: Full Freedom for Former Australian Detainee
2008-12-21.abstract: David Hicks became a completely free man Sunday morning, for the first time since being picked up by the Americans in Afghanistan seven years ago this month.

2008-12-22.title: Protests Erupt in Russia Over Raising of Car Tariffs
2008-12-22.abstract: Small demonstrations occurred in several cities over the weekend in reaction to the government’s plan to raise tariffs on imported automobiles.

2008-12-22.title: Iranian Police Shut Down Rights Office
2008-12-22.abstract: The police raided and shut down the office of Iran’s leading human rights group, hours before it was to celebrate the 60th anniversary of Human Rights Day.

2008-12-22.title: Tehran Says It’s Getting Missiles
2008-12-22.abstract: An Iranian official said Russia had started delivering an advanced air-defense system to Iran, despite earlier denials by Russia that a deal had been reached.

2008-12-22.title: Redefining the Role of the U.S. Military in Iraq
2008-12-22.abstract: Military planners are quietly acknowledging that many soldiers will stay in Iraq as “trainers” and “advisers.”

2008-12-22.title: In Zimbabwe, Survival Lies in Scavenging
2008-12-22.abstract: Catastrophic agricultural policies and economic collapse have left the country haunted by the half-starved.

2008-12-23.title: Ghana’s Image, Glowing Abroad, Is Beginning to Show a Few Blemishes at Home
2008-12-23.abstract: Some in Ghana worry that the country’s image as a bastion of peace and democracy is merely a sign of the low expectations outsiders have for Africa.

2008-12-23.title: Security Council Extends Protection of Iraq’s Assets
2008-12-23.abstract: The resolution effectively shields Iraq’s oil and other revenues from billions of dollars in international claims stemming from the era of Saddam Hussein.

2008-12-23.title: The Doors Shut on an Emporium Offering a Hodgepodge of Essentials
2008-12-23.abstract: After trying unsuccessfully to find a buyer in a crashing economy, Woolworths announced last week that it was shutting all of its 807 stores in Britain by Jan. 5.

2008-12-23.title: A War Still Seen Through Stained Glass
2008-12-23.abstract: The 66-foot-tall windows that Russia returned to Germany last month are a symbol of what remains to be done to untangle the legacies of Nazism and Communism.

2008-12-23.title: Zimbabwe: U.N. Sends Supplies
2008-12-23.abstract: Unicef airlifted emergency supplies to Zimbabwe on Monday to combat cholera and to aid pregnant women, who are at risk because of the country’s collapsing health system.

2008-12-24.title: Jordanian Students Rebel, Embracing Conservative Islam
2008-12-24.abstract: Across the Middle East, young people are using religion to defy the status quo and challenge their governments.

2008-12-24.title: Where a Yule Log Is So Much More Than Christmas Cake
2008-12-24.abstract: In Paris, the cake known as the Bûche de Noël is an opportunity for creativity, commerce, competition and consumption.

2008-12-24.title: Russia’s Liberals Lose Their Voice
2008-12-24.abstract: Tired of being hounded, a prominent opposition leader decided to accept a job with the Kremlin.

2008-12-24.title: Afghans and U.S. Plan to Recruit Local Militias
2008-12-24.abstract: American commanders and Afghan leaders hope to imitate Iraq’s successful experiment, but there are fears it could prompt further bloodletting.

2008-12-24.title: Pakistan Says Banned Group Helped Plan Attack on Hotel
2008-12-24.abstract: A top official said Lashkar-e-Jhangvi, a Sunni Muslim extremist group, helped carry out the Marriot Hotel bombing in Islamabad three months ago.

2008-12-25.title: A Foreign Face Beloved by Afghans of All Stripes
2008-12-25.abstract: Afghans of all ethnic and political stripes, even the Taliban, seem likely to count Alberto Cairo as one foreigner who left the country better than he found it.

2008-12-25.title: Cash Flow From Tajik Migrants Stalls
2008-12-25.abstract: Countries like Tajikistan that have come to depend on remittances are feeling the effects of the global financial crisis.

2008-12-25.title: Amid Taliban Rule, a NATO Supply Line Is Choked
2008-12-25.abstract: The downward spiral of a region east of the Khyber Pass is sending American military officials scrambling to find alternative routes into Afghanistan.

2008-12-25.title: A Mexican Celebration of Christ and Community
2008-12-25.abstract: In few places, perhaps, does the Christmas season unite whole villages so thoroughly in communal rituals of music and merrymaking as in rural Mexico.

2008-12-25.title: Estonia Spy Case Rattles Nerves at NATO
2008-12-25.abstract: The case of an Estonian police official accused of being a spy has stirred up fears that Russian intelligence networks may survive, in dormant form, even at the highest levels of government.

2008-12-26.title: Newfound Riches Come With Spiritual Costs for Turkey’s Religious Merchants
2008-12-26.abstract: While other Muslim societies are wrestling with radicals, Turkey’s religious merchant class is struggling instead with wealth.

2008-12-26.title: Sudan’s Tug of War Over City Puts Treaty at Risk
2008-12-26.abstract: The market town of Abyei serves as kind of a fulcrum balancing perhaps the most important peace treaty in Africa.

2008-12-26.title: Iraq Unsettled by Political Power Plays
2008-12-26.abstract: Different factions within the Iraqi government — and some outside it — are struggling to gain ground as U.S. influence wanes.

2008-12-26.title: In a Teeming French City, Safe Harbor at the Movies
2008-12-26.abstract: A theater is flourishing in a poor, potentially volatile district of the French port city of Marseille.

2008-12-26.title: In Mosul, Iraqi Christians Brave the Violence to Celebrate Christmas
2008-12-26.abstract: Mosul remains one of the most dangerous places in Iraq. And on Thursday, people there prayed for their safety.

2008-12-27.title: No Furnaces but Heat Aplenty in ‘Passive Houses’
2008-12-27.abstract: Innovative homes pioneered in Germany are encased in such an airtight shell that barely any heat escapes.

2008-12-27.title: Islamic Revival Tests Bosnia’s Secular Cast
2008-12-27.abstract: Islam is making a comeback in Bosnia, where religious and national identities have long been fused.

2008-12-27.title: Behind Analyst’s Cool Demeanor, Deep Anxiety Over American Policy
2008-12-27.abstract: Former Middle East analyst Bruce Riedel is one of a chorus of terrorism experts who see Al Qaeda’s base in the mountains of Pakistan as America’s greatest threat.

2008-12-27.title: Afghanistan: Protests Over Raid
2008-12-27.abstract: For the second time in a little over a week, a deadly United States military raid on an Afghan house has incited protests and produced conflicting reports over who was killed.

2008-12-27.title: Israelis Say Strikes Against Hamas Will Continue
2008-12-27.abstract: Israeli officials said the Gaza airstrikes, which killed more than 225 on Saturday and went on into the night, were the start of what could be days or even months of an effort against Hamas.

2008-12-28.title: White House Puts Onus on Hamas to End Violence
2008-12-28.abstract: The Bush administration issued blistering criticism of Hamas, saying the group had provoked Israel’s airstrikes on Gaza by firing rockets into southern Israel.

2008-12-28.title: Israeli Troops Mass Along Border; Arab Anger Rises
2008-12-28.abstract: With the death toll in Gaza rising to nearly 300, a furious reaction spread across the Arab world, raising fears of greater instability in the region.

2008-12-28.title: Foreign Minister of Israel Says Hamas Should Be Condemned
2008-12-28.abstract: Tzipi Livni defended the Israeli assault in Gaza, saying on America’s Sunday morning talk shows that Hamas, not Israel, “is the one who needs to be condemned.”

2008-12-28.title: Violence Continues in Gaza
2008-12-28.abstract: Israel’s assault on Hamas in the Gaza Strip continued on Sunday, with warplanes dropping bombs on tunnels that were considered a weapons pipeline.

2008-12-28.title: Blast Kills 16 Afghans Near Pakistan Border
2008-12-28.abstract: A suicide bombing on Sunday killed at least 16, including 13 schoolchildren, and wounded 53 in Khost Province near the border with Pakistan, local officials said.

2008-12-29.title: No Easy Indian Response to Pakistan’s Troop Shift
2008-12-29.abstract: Policy makers and analysts say an attack on Pakistan could damage India’s economic prospects at a time when the country is already vulnerable to the global downturn.

2008-12-29.title: Romance and Recovery in Quake Area
2008-12-29.abstract: Months after an earthquake hit southwestern China, new couples are one sign of the beginnings of a recovery.

2008-12-29.title: Islamist Militants in Somalia Begin to Fight One Another
2008-12-29.abstract: A newly militarized Islamist group, the Ahlu-Sunna Wal-Jama, killed more than 10 fighters from the Shabab, a rival Islamist faction that was known as one of Somalia’s toughest.

2008-12-29.title: Obama Defers to Bush, for Now, on Gaza Crisis
2008-12-29.abstract: The attack presents the president-elect with yet another foreign crisis to deal with when he enters the White House, even as he has struggled to focus on the country’s economic problems.

2008-12-29.title: Israel Reminds Foes That It Has Teeth
2008-12-29.abstract: Israel’s military operation in Gaza aims to expunge the ghost of its flawed 2006 war in Lebanon and re-establish its deterrence.

2008-12-30.title: After 4 Years, Switzerland Frees Man Suspected of Smuggling Nuclear Technology
2008-12-30.abstract: The brother and father of Urs Tinner, who spent four years in detention for his connection to the nuclear black market of Abdul Qadeer Khan, are also connected to the Swiss investigation.

2008-12-30.title: Rare Suicide Bombing in Iran Kills 4
2008-12-30.abstract: The Sunni militant group to which the suicide bomber was affiliated had attacked Iranian armed forces and Revolutionary Guards but not through suicide bombings.

2008-12-30.title: China: Hu Visits Quake Area Again
2008-12-30.abstract: China’s president, Hu Jintao visited a region of southwest China over the weekend that was hit hard by the earthquake last May.

2008-12-30.title: Beacon of Japan’s Future, Sparkling With Nostalgia
2008-12-30.abstract: Tokyo Tower, the city’s Eiffel Tower-like structure, has turned 50, and has become the symbol of a bygone era that has won a new place in the national imagination.

2008-12-30.title: Iranian Authorities Raid Offices of Rights Advocate
2008-12-30.abstract: Authorities stormed the offices of Shirin Ebadi, the country’s most prominent human rights activist and a Nobel Prize winner, seizing computers and documents.

2008-12-31.title: New Rules in Iraq Add Police Work to Troops’ Jobs
2008-12-31.abstract: After Jan. 1, American soldiers will be required to secure arrest warrants in order to detain Iraqi citizens.

2008-12-31.title: Russia Aside, Georgia’s Chief Is Pressed at Home
2008-12-31.abstract: Mikheil Saakashvili is facing pressure from Russia and domestic rivals at a time when an economic downturn could upend Georgia’s political order.

2008-12-31.title: As a German Neighborhood Changes, a Raunchy Shadow Lingers
2008-12-31.abstract: The recent reincarnation of a magazine known for its provocative mixture of satire, sex and left-wing politics is the latest sign of a neighborhood going rapidly upscale.

2008-12-31.title: Moderate Arab States Feel Popular Anger
2008-12-31.abstract: The violence in Gaza has put Egypt, Jordan and Saudi Arabia on the defensive, while Syria and Iran have drawn praise in the Arab world for their militancy.

2008-12-31.title: Iranian President Proposes Ending Energy Subsidies
2008-12-31.abstract: Faced with falling oil prices and a weakening economy, President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad proposed what would be a major change of course for the oil-producing country.
